title: 10 best herbal teas for red eyes

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Kansas Coneflower tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes associated with redness.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in Kansas Coneflower tea can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness and irritation.

Soothing properties: The saponins and other bioactive compounds in Kansas Coneflower tea have a calming effect on the mucous membranes and tissues of the eyes, helping to soothe and relieve discomfort associated with red eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe irritated tissues. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Echinacea angustifolia have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate redness, making it a popular natural remedy for red, itchy, or irritated eyes. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage, promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds may help reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which could contribute to soothing red, irritated eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which might help alleviate redness and swelling in the eyes.
Alkaloids: Some Echinacea alkaloids, like echinacoside, may possess anti-inflammatory properties that could aid in reducing redness and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their mild properties and ability to complement the effects of the roots, providing a more balanced and soothing effect.
Flowers: They are used for their potential to help reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes, as well as their mild antihistamine properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried root of echinacea angustifolia, typically 1 to 2 teaspoons per cup of water.
Combine the dried root with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes, allowing the flavors and active compounds to infuse.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solid root particles.
Serve the tea hot or cold, garnished with a slice of lemon or honey if desired.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes by decreasing inflammation.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, including beta-carotene and lutein, protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to red eye symptoms like dryness and irritation.

Antimicrobial property: Pot marigold's antimicrobial compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help combat infections that can cause red eyes by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues, providing relief from redness and puffiness. Calendula's natural antiseptic properties also help to combat infection and promote healing, making it a popular remedy for red, irritated eyes. As a result, Calendula tea may be an effective way to alleviate red eye discomfort and promote overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Tannins: Tannins in Calendula officinalis teas can help constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which may alleviate redness and discomfort in the eyes.

Nascent triterpene acids: These acids, particularly those in the form of oleanolic acid, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and calm red eyes.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and saponins that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them suitable for treating red, irritated eyes.
Stems: The stems, like the flowers and leaves, contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Rinse the flowers in cold water and pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Steep one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or three to four tablespoons of fresh flowers in boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before serving or refrigerating for later use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile tea contains apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in chamomile tea help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness and irritation.
Soothing flavonoids: Chamomile tea's flavonoid content helps to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing itchiness, burning, and discomfort associated with red eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in chamomile tea have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate irritation in the eyes. This makes it a popular remedy for red, itchy, and puffy eyes. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects can calm the eyes and provide relief from stress and fatigue, which can often exacerbate red eye symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with red eyes, promoting relaxation and soothing of the ocular area.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate redness and swelling in the eyes.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, providing relief from redness and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, they help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and discomfort.
Seeds: Although not as commonly used as flowers, seeds are sometimes used to make teas due to their potential to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning when the dew is still present for best quality.
Dry the harvested matricaria chamomilla flowers in a single layer at 40°c for 2 hours.
Measure out 1 tablespoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving the herbal tea hot or iced immediately.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tea contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, helping to alleviate redness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea, such as quercetin and luteolin, can help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, contributing to redness.

Vasodilatory properties: Dandelion tea's ability to dilate blood vessels can help increase blood flow to the eyes, which may reduce redness by increasing oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the affected area.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's root and leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to reduce swelling and combat oxidative stress that can cause eye redness. Additionally, dandelion teas are rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate redness. The soothing and calming effects of Taraxacum officinale teas make it a popular natural remedy for relieving irritated and red eyes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by allergies or irritations.

Luteolin: As a flavonoid, luteolin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate redness and discomfort in the eyes by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation.
Apigenin: This flavonoid has a relaxing effect on the muscles and blood vessels, which can help reduce eye strain and alleviate redness caused by fatigue or overuse.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling.
Flowers: Used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which calm and decongest the eyes.
Roots: Used for their diuretic properties, which help to reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at dawn when they are at their most potent and fragrant.
Dry the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers for every 8 ounces of water.
Steep the dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the hot water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve immediately for the best flavor and potency in 3 to 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Golden seal tea contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to redness and irritation in the eyes.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: The alkaloids berberine and hydrastine present in golden seal tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from conditions like conjunctivitis and pink eye.
Antimicrobial Properties: Golden seal tea's antimicrobial compounds can help combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections that may be contributing to red eyes, such as blepharitis or styes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, teas helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The berberine alkaloids present in goldenseal have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, which can help alleviate redness and discomfort in the eyes. Additionally, goldenseal's antiseptic properties can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to red eyes, promoting a speedy recovery and relief from symptoms. Regular consumption of goldenseal tea may also help to strengthen the eyes' natural defense mechanisms.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and redness associated with red eyes.
Alkaloids (primarily Berberine and Palmitoylberberine): Have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to red eyes.
Flavonoids (primarily Quercetin): Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The main component used for making teas for red eyes is due to its high content of berberine, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a higher concentration of hydrastine, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are utilized for teas due to their content of berberine and hydrastine, which work together to alleviate redness and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried hydrastis canadensis root and grind it into a fine powder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of ground root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove any solids.
Add honey to taste if desired to enhance the flavor of the tea.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before consuming.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and redness associated with allergies, conjunctivitis, or dry eye.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in eyebright tea neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes' delicate tissues, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall eye health.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Eyebright's antimicrobial compounds help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause red, itchy, or watery eyes, promoting a healthy balance of microflora in the eyes and surrounding areas.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, teas helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, have been found to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, soothing redness and irritation. Eyebright's ability to constrict blood vessels also helps to decrease the appearance of redness and puffiness. As a natural remedy, eyebright tea has been used for centuries to alleviate eye discomfort and promote healthy vision. Its soothing effects make it a popular choice for addressing red, irritated eyes.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help to reduce inflammation and soothe the eyes by exerting their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
Alkaloids: They act as vasoconstrictors, which can help reduce blood flow to the eyes, thereby alleviating redness and swelling.
Flavonoids (specifically Rutin): They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and protect the eyes from oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to reduce eye inflammation and redness.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Buds: The buds contain flavonoids and other compounds that are believed to help reduce swelling and ease eye strain, making them a popular choice for tea blends.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of euphrasia officinalis in the early morning or late afternoon when the plant is less prone to drying.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 25-30 degrees celsius for 24-48 hours to preserve their potency.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried euphrasia officinalis leaves for each 8 ounce cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried leaves in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release the herb's medicinal properties.
Strain the tea and discard the solids after 5-10 minutes to enjoy the herbal infusion.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon verbena tea contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes, making it an effective remedy for red, irritated eyes.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in lemon verbena tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, can help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, reducing redness and irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena officinalis, also known as lemon verbena, teas helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, its antioxidant and soothing properties help calm the eye tissues, reducing redness and discomfort. Furthermore, Verbena officinalis tea's natural decongestant properties help to clear nasal passages, which can contribute to red and irritated eyes, making it a beneficial tea for overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Verbascoside: Verbascoside is a secoiridoid glycoside that helps with red eyes by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, which in turn alleviates the symptoms of conjunctivitis and other eye irritations.
Limonene: Limonene is a terpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe red eyes and reduce swelling caused by allergies or irritations.

Verbena aldehyde: Verbena aldehyde is an iridoid compound with antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles around the eyes, thereby reducing redness and discomfort caused by eye strain or irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas for red eyes because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
Flowers: They are used to make teas for red eyes because of their calming and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce redness and promote relaxation.
Roots: They are used to make teas for red eyes because of their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers just before brewing for optimal flavor and aroma.
Dry the harvested verbena leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried verbena leaves and flowers per 8 ounces of boiling water for a standard infusion.
Steep the dried verbena leaves and flowers in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the desired compounds.
Strain the verbena tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving.

remedy_properties: Caffeoylquinic acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by allergies or irritants.
Berberine: As an antibacterial agent, berberine can help combat infections that may be causing eye irritation, such as conjunctivitis, and promote healing.
Sodium ferulate: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye inflammation and protect the eyes from further damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corydalis yanhusuo, also known as yanhusuo, teas helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and vasodilating properties. Its ability to relax the blood vessels surrounding the eyes also contributes to its effectiveness in reducing redness and promoting overall eye health. Corydaline: Corydaline has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate pain and reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from eye irritations.

Yanhusuo Alkaloids (e.g., Tetrahydropalmatine): Yanhusuo alkaloids have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes by improving blood flow and alleviating inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Corydalis yanhusuo are primarily used in teas for red eyes due to their high content of tetradrine, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Corydalis yanhusuo leaves are used in teas for red eyes because they contain berberine, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that help alleviate eye discomfort.

Rhyzomes: Corydalis yanhusuo rhyzomes are used in teas for red eyes due to their rich content of corydaline, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 3-6 grams of dried corydalis yanhusuo root.
Combine measured corydalis yanhusuo root with 1 liter of water in a medium saucepan.
Bring the water to a boil over high heat then reduce to a simmer.
Steep for 30-60 minutes or according to desired tea strength.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: English Lavender tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which can help reduce swelling and relieve discomfort associated with red eyes.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the eyes from free radical damage caused by environmental stressors, UV rays, or other factors that contribute to redness.

Flowers: The flowers are used because of their calming and antispasmodic effects, which can help reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes.
Buds: Buds are used for their mild sedative properties, which can help relax the muscles around the eyes and reduce redness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in quantities of 1 to 2 teaspoons per 8 ounces of water.
Steep the harvested flowers in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the tea mixture to remove the lavandula angustifolia flowers from the liquid.
Add honey or lemon to taste and adjust the sweetness and flavor as needed.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow tea contains flavonoids and terpenes that reduce swelling and inflammation, which can help alleviate redness and discomfort in the eyes.
Antioxidant activity: Yarrow's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness and irritation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a cool place for at least 24 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids immediately.
Drink the tea hot or cold within 30 minutes of preparation to maximize the benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod teas helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated eye tissues and reduce swelling. The herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to calm the conjunctiva, the thin membrane covering the white part of the eye, and ease discomfort caused by allergies, dryness, or exposure to irritants. As a result, goldenrod teas can provide quick relief from red, itchy, and watery eyes, leaving them feeling refreshed and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Goldenrod tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of red eye.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in goldenrod tea can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the likelihood of inflammation and redness.
Astringent properties: Goldenrod's astringent compounds can help constrict blood vessels and reduce excess moisture, which can help alleviate redness and irritation in the eyes.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried solidago virgaurea flowers for every 8 ounces of water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a medium-sized pot and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the solidago virgaurea flowers to the boiling water and allow them to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature, then drink it as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail teas helps with red eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation. The tea's natural antioxidants also work to calm and soothe the delicate tissues around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. Additionally, field horsetail contains silicon, a mineral that can help to strengthen the tiny blood vessels in the eyes, reducing their appearance of being inflamed or damaged. Rhyzomes: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate redness and itching in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality equisetum arvense leaves and stems, rinsing them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the harvested equisetum arvense into small pieces, using a pair of scissors or pruning shears.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped equisetum arvense with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a medium-sized pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse and release their benefits.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and serving hot or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. When brewed into a warm, comforting drink, these compounds help to calm the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes, alleviating symptoms such as itchiness, burning, and redness associated with dry or irritated eyes.

Barks: Containing natural astringents, helps constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, providing relief from red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or extract per cup of boiling water.
Steep the ginkgo biloba in hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with red eyes because its soothing properties can effectively calm irritated eye tissues. The mucilages present in marshmallow roots work wonders by reducing inflammation, puffiness, and discomfort associated with red eyes caused by dryness, allergy, or fatigue. As the tea is absorbed into the bloodstream, it cools and moisturizes the eyes from the inside out, providing a refreshing and calming sensation that can help alleviate redness and leave your peepers feeling refreshed and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow tea's mucilage content helps soothe and lubricate dry, irritated eyes by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the eye.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in marshmallow tea reduce swelling and redness in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Antioxidant activity: Marshmallow tea's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye irritation, promoting overall eye health.

Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Cut the cleaned marshmallow roots into small pieces and combine one teaspoon of root with two cups of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten minutes, allowing the herbal extract to dissolve fully in the hot water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and leaving only the liquid.
Allow the cooled tea to steep for at least 30 minutes before refrigerating it for later use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of sambucus nigra in quantities of 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of water.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to red eyes that are treated with herbal teas are conjunctivitis, dry eyes, and blepharitis. intro_desc: Herbal teas for red eyes are a natural remedy designed to alleviate and soothe red, itchy, and irritated eyes, caused by factors such as dryness, allergies, or fatigue. These teas are effective in reducing inflammation and promoting eye health. The best herbal teas for red eyes include Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Achillea millefolium, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe barbadensis, Echinacea purpurea, Chamomilla recutita, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Aloe vera. If left untreated, red eyes can lead to chronic discomfort, impaired vision, and even affect work productivity, daily routines, and social interactions, ultimately impacting overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with red eyes is a blend of peppermint, chamomile, and calendula. Peppermint tea helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the eyes, while chamomile tea calms the mind and body, reducing stress that can exacerbate red eyes. Calendula tea, rich in antioxidants, promotes healing and protects the delicate skin around the eyes. Together, these teas work to alleviate redness, itchiness, and discomfort, providing long-lasting relief and promoting overall eye health.
